messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following dating app privacy policy for GDPR compliance:

"LoveLink collects your name, age, photos, sexual orientation, relationship preferences, and location to match you with compatible partners. We obtain explicit consent for all personal data including sensitive categories. You can update or delete your profile at any time. We use AWS US East servers. Data is retained while your account is active."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PARTIAL] Explicit consent stated, implying Art. 6(1)(a). The lawful basis should be stated explicitly rather than implied.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Sexual orientation is explicitly collected and is Art. 9 special category data. Explicit consent is claimed but the mechanism is not described — consent for special category data must be granular and separately obtained.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Partner matching is a clear and specific purpose.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PARTIAL] Location data scope (real-time vs. city-level) is not defined.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PARTIAL] Retention while account is active is stated but no post-deletion erasure timeframe is given.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PARTIAL] Profile update (rectification) and deletion (erasure) referenced. Access, portability, restriction, and objection rights not stated.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [FAIL] No processors or third parties disclosed beyond AWS.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [FAIL] AWS US East disclosed but no transfer safeguard mechanism stated.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [FAIL] Not addressed. Large-scale processing of sexual orientation and location data almost certainly requires a DPO.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [FAIL] Not addressed.

VERDICT: Non-Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 9 (special category consent mechanism not adequately described), Art. 5 (location scope undefined, no post-deletion retention period), Art. 15-21 (most rights absent), Art. 28 (processors not disclosed), Art. 46 (US transfer safeguards absent), Art. 37 (DPO required), Art. 33 (no breach notification)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Separate consent for sexual orientation (Art. 9) from general consent with explicit granular mechanism
- Define location data scope
- Add post-account-deletion data erasure timeframe
- Add full data subject rights section
- Disclose all sub-processors
- Reference AWS SCCs as transfer safeguard
- Appoint and disclose DPO
- Add breach notification commitment
